key_insight: WarpagePINN uses a two-stage physics-informed neural network to predict chiplet temperature and thermal warpage without labeled simulation data, while embedding the governing physics directly into training.
engineering_relevance: It offers a fast, accurate surrogate for advanced packaging design where CTE mismatch drives deformation, enabling rapid warpage assessment and uncertainty studies.
method: Stage 1: Fourier-series temperature model trained only from the heat-equation loss, with boundary conditions built in.
Stage 2: MLP warpage predictor trained with a hybrid supervisory strategy that optimizes an energy-based loss.
Parametric extension models CTE uncertainty for sensitivity and design-space exploration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement with finite element results, a mean absolute error of 0.2 μm, and about a 1000× speedup in CTE parameterization studies.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the physics assumptions, boundary conditions, and CTE parameter ranges match real packaging configurations.
paper_title: WarpagePINN: Thermal Warpage Prediction in Advanced Packaging via a Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Networks
